Subsection.Text: Subsection 551(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An authorized foreign bank must not make a loan in Canada on the security of residential property in Canada for the purpose of purchasing, renovating or improving that residential property, or refinance a loan for that purpose, if the amount of the loan, together with the amount outstanding of any mortgage or hypothec of equal or higher rank on the residential property, would exceed 80% of the value of the residential property at the time of the loan.

An authorized foreign bank shall not make a loan in Canada on the security of residential property in Canada for the purpose of purchasing, renovating or improving that property, or refinance a loan for that purpose, if the amount of the loan, together with the amount outstanding of any mortgage having an equal or prior claim against the property, would exceed 80 per cent of the value of the property at the time of the loan.
Paragraph 551(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made or guaranteed under the National Housing Act or any other Act of Parliament by or under which a different limit on the value of residential property on the security of which the authorized foreign bank may make a loan is established;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made or guaranteed under the National Housing Act or any other Act of Parliament by or under which a different limit on the value of property on the security of which the authorized foreign bank may make a loan is established;
脗聽

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a loan secured by a mortgage where

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the mortgage is taken back by the authorized foreign bank on a property disposed of by it, including where the disposition is by way of a realization of a security interest, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the mortgage secures payment of an amount payable to the authorized foreign bank for the property.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 551(2)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a loan secured by a mortgage or hypothec if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the mortgage or hypothec is taken back by the authorized foreign bank on a property disposed of by it, including when the disposition is by way of a realization of a security interest, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the mortgage or hypothec secures payment of an amount payable to the authorized foreign bank for the property.

Section.Text: Section 553 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

An authorized foreign bank must not, in respect of its business in Canada, grant to a person the right to appoint a receiver, receiver and manager or sequestrator of the property or of the business of the authorized foreign bank.

An authorized foreign bank shall not, in respect of its business in Canada, grant to a person the right to appoint a receiver or a receiver and manager of the property or of the business of the authorized foreign bank.
Subsection 556(4) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Le paragraphe (3) s芒聙聶applique que la fiducie soit expresse ou par effet de la loi et m脙陋me si la banque 脙漏trang脙篓re autoris脙漏e en a 脙漏t脙漏 avis脙漏e si elle agit sur l芒聙聶ordre ou sous l芒聙聶autorit脙漏 du ou des titulaires du compte dans lequel le d脙漏p脙麓t est effectu脙漏.

Subsection (3) applies regardless of whether the trust is express or arises by the operation of law, and it applies even when the authorized foreign bank has notice of the trust if it acts on the order of or under the authority of the holder or holders of the account into which the deposit is made.

Section.Text: Subsection 577(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the transmission of a debt owing by an authorized foreign bank by reason of a deposit, of property held by an authorized foreign bank as security or for safekeeping or of rights with respect to a safety deposit box and property deposited in it takes place because of the death of a person, the delivery to the authorized foreign bank of the following is sufficient justification and authority for giving effect to the transmission:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an affidavit or declaration in writing that is in a form satisfactory to the authorized foreign bank, that is signed by or on behalf of a person claiming by virtue of the transmission and that states the nature and effect of the transmission; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: one of the following documents:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the claim is based on a will or other testamentary instrument, on a grant of probate of a will or other testamentary instrument with or without letters testamentary or other document of similar import, on a grant of letters of administration or other document of similar import, on a judgment probating a will or other document of similar import, on letters of verification or other document of similar import, or on a certified copy of the notarial minutes of probate of a will or other document of similar import, purporting to be issued by any court or authority in Canada or elsewhere, an authenticated copy or certificate of the document under the seal of the court or authority without proof of the authenticity of the seal or other proof, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the claim is based on a notarial will, an authentic copy of the notarial will.

Subsection.Text: Where the transmission of a debt owing by an authorized foreign bank by reason of a deposit, of property held by an authorized foreign bank as security or for safe-keeping or of rights with respect to a safety deposit box and property deposited in it takes place because of the death of a person, the delivery to the authorized foreign bank of the following is sufficient justification and authority for giving effect to the transmission in accordance with the claim:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an affidavit or declaration in writing in form satisfactory to the authorized foreign bank signed by or on behalf of a person claiming by virtue of the transmission stating the nature and effect of the transmission; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: one of the following, namely,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the claim is based on a will or other testamentary instrument or on a grant of probate of a will or testamentary instrument or on such a grant and letters testamentary or other document of like import or on a grant of letters of administration or other document of like import, purporting to be issued by any court or authority in Canada or elsewhere, an authenticated copy or certificate of the document under the seal of the court or authority without proof of the authenticity of the seal or other proof, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the claim is based on a notarial will, an authenticated copy of the notarial will.

Subsection.Text: When a director of a bank holding company pays a debt referred to in subsection (1) that is proven in liquidation and dissolution or bankruptcy proceedings, the director is entitled to any preference that the employee would have been entitled to and, if a judgment has been obtained, the director is

Paragraph.Text: in Quebec, subrogated to the employee芒聙聶s rights as declared in the judgment; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: elsewhere in Canada, entitled to an assignment of the judgment.

Subsection.Text: Where a director of a bank holding company pays a debt referred to in subsection (1) that is proven in liquidation and dissolution or bankruptcy proceedings, the director is entitled to any preference that the employee would have been entitled to and, where a judgment has been obtained, the director is entitled to an assignment of the judgment.

Section.Text: Subsection 810(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a bank holding company or any director, any officer, any employee or any agent or mandatary of a bank holding company is contravening or has failed to comply with any term or condition made in respect of the issuance of letters patent of amalgamation, the Minister may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order directing the bank holding company or the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary to comply with the term or condition, cease the contravention or do any thing that is required to be done, and on the application the court may so order and make any other order it thinks fit.

If a bank holding company or any director, officer, employee or agent of a bank holding company is contravening or has failed to comply with any term or condition made in respect of the issuance of letters patent of amalgamation, the Minister may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order directing the bank holding company or the director, officer, employee or agent to comply with the term or condition, cease the contravention or do any thing that is required to be done, and on the application the court may so order and make any other order it thinks fit.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 817(5) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person requiring a bank holding company to supply a basic list of shareholders may, if the person states in the accompanying affidavit that supplemental lists are required, request the bank holding company or its agent or mandatary, on payment of a reasonable fee, to provide supplemental lists of shareholders setting out any changes from the basic list in the names and addresses of the shareholders and the number of shares owned by each shareholder for each business day following the date to which the basic list is made up.

A person requiring a bank holding company to supply a basic list of shareholders may, if the person states in the accompanying affidavit that supplemental lists are required, request the bank holding company or its agent, on payment of a reasonable fee, to provide supplemental lists of shareholders setting out any changes from the basic list in the names and addresses of the shareholders and the number of shares owned by each shareholder for each business day following the date to which the basic list is made up.
The portion of subsection 817(6) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: When supplemental lists to be furnished
Subsection.Text: A bank holding company or its agent or mandatary must provide a supplemental list of shareholders required under subsection (5)
A bank holding company or its agent shall provide a supplemental list of shareholders required under subsection (5)

A bank holding company and its agents or mandataries must take reasonable precautions to

Subsection.Text: Subsection 825(6) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person who wishes to examine the central securities register, take extracts from it or have copies of it made may on payment of a reasonable fee, if they state in the accompanying affidavit that supplementary information is required, request the bank holding company or its agent or mandatary to provide supplementary information setting out any changes made to the register.

A person who wishes to examine a central securities register, take extracts from it or have copies of it made may on payment of a reasonable fee, if they state in the accompanying affidavit that supplementary information is required, request the bank holding company or its agent to provide supplementary information setting out any changes made to the register.
The portion of subsection 825(7) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

A bank holding company or its agent shall provide the supplementary information within

A bank holding company may appoint an agent or mandatary to maintain its central securities register and each of its branch securities registers.
A bank holding company may appoint an agent to maintain its central securities register and each of its branch securities registers.
The portion of section 831 of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A bank holding company, its agent or mandatary or a trustee as defined in section 294 is not required to produce

A document executed on behalf of a bank holding company is not invalid merely because a corporate seal is not affixed to it.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 856(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Section.Text: No person may control or be a major shareholder of a bank holding company if the person or an entity affiliated with the person

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: engages in Canada in any personal property leasing activity that a financial leasing entity within the meaning of subsection 464(1) is prohibited from engaging in.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 886(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: control or have a substantial investment in an entity that engages in Canada in any personal property or movable leasing activity that a financial leasing entity as defined in subsection 464(1) is prohibited from engaging in; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: engage in Canada in any personal property or movable leasing activity that a financial leasing entity as defined in subsection 464(1) is prohibited from engaging in.

Section.Text: No person who controls a bank holding company or who is a major shareholder of a bank holding company, and no entity affiliated with that person, may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: control or have a substantial investment in an entity that engages in Canada in any personal property leasing activity that a financial leasing entity within the meaning of subsection 464(1) is prohibited from engaging in; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: engage in Canada in any personal property leasing activity that a financial leasing entity within the meaning of subsection 464(1) is prohibited from engaging in.

Section.Text: Section 920 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A bank holding company and any person who is a director, an officer, an employee or an agent or mandatary of the bank holding company may rely on any information contained in a declaration required by the directors under section 919 or on any information otherwise acquired in respect of any matter that might be the subject of a declaration under section 919, and no action lies against the bank holding company or the person for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in reliance on the information.

A bank holding company and any person who is a director or an officer, employee or agent of the bank holding company may rely on any information contained in a declaration required by the directors pursuant to section 919 or on any information otherwise acquired in respect of any matter that might be the subject of such a declaration, and no action lies against the bank holding company or any such person for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in reliance on any such information.

Section.Text: Section 938 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

A bank holding company must not, and must not permit its prescribed subsidiaries to, purchase or otherwise acquire an interest in real property or make an improvement to any real property or immovable in respect of which the bank holding company or any of its prescribed subsidiaries has an interest in real property if the aggregate value of all interests of the bank holding company in real property exceeds, or the acquisition of the interest or the making of the improvement would cause that aggregate value to exceed, the prescribed percentage of the regulatory capital of the bank holding company.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 155:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of the heading and section 938:

Section.Text: A bank holding company shall not, and shall not permit its prescribed subsidiaries to, purchase or otherwise acquire an interest in real property or make an improvement to any real property in which the bank holding company or any of its prescribed subsidiaries has an interest if the aggregate value of all interests of the bank holding company in real property exceeds, or the acquisition of the interest or the making of the improvement would cause that aggregate value to exceed, the prescribed percentage of the regulatory capital of the bank holding company.

Section.Text: Section 986 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an entity commits an offence under this Act, any director, any officer, any agent or mandatary or any principal officer of the entity who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to and guilty of the offence and liable on summary conviction or on conviction on indictment to the punishment provided under paragraph 985(1)(a) for the offence, whether or not the entity has been prosecuted or convicted.

If an entity commits an offence under this Act, any officer, director, agent or principal officer of the entity who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to and guilty of the offence and liable on summary conviction or on conviction on indictment to the punishment provided under paragraph 985(1)(a) for the offence, whether or not the entity has been prosecuted or convicted.
Section 989 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Compliance or restraining order 芒聙聰 bank

Subsection.Text: If a bank or a bank holding company or any director, any officer, any employee or any agent or mandatary of one does not comply with any provision of this Act or the regulations other than a consumer provision, or of the incorporating instrument or any by-law of the bank or bank holding company, the Superintendent, any complainant or any creditor of the bank or bank holding company may, in addition to any other right that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the bank, the bank holding company, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the bank, the bank holding company, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Compliance or restraining order 芒聙聰 authorized foreign bank
Subsection.Text: If an authorized foreign bank or any of its directors, its officers, its employees or its agents or mandataries does not comply with any provision of this Act or the regulations other than a consumer provision, or of an order made under subsection 524(1), 528(1) or (1.1) or 534(1) in respect of the authorized foreign bank, the Superintendent, any complainant or any creditor of the authorized foreign bank may, in addition to any other right that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the authorized foreign bank, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the authorized foreign bank, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Compliance or restraining order 芒聙聰 consumer provisions
Subsection.Text: If a bank or an authorized foreign bank or any director, any officer, any employee or any agent or mandatary of one does not comply with any applicable consumer provision, the Commissioner or any complainant may, in addition to any other right that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the bank, the authorized foreign bank, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the bank, the authorized foreign bank, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the consumer provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

If a bank or a bank holding company or any director, officer, employee or agent of one does not comply with any provision of this Act or the regulations other than a consumer provision, or of the incorporating instrument or any by-law of the bank or bank holding company, the Superintendent, any complainant or any creditor of the bank or bank holding company may, in addition to any other right that that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the bank, bank holding company, director, officer, employee or agent to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the bank, bank holding company, director, officer, employee or agent from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

If an authorized foreign bank or any of its directors, officers, employees or agents does not comply with any provision of this Act or the regulations other than a consumer provision, or of an order made under subsection 524(1), 528(1) or (1.1) or 534(1) in respect of the authorized foreign bank, the Superintendent, any complainant or any creditor of the authorized foreign bank may, in addition to any other right that that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the authorized foreign bank, director, officer, employee or agent to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the authorized foreign bank, director, officer, employee or agent from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order that it thinks fit.

If a bank or an authorized foreign bank or any director, officer, employee or agent of one does not comply with any applicable consumer provision, the Commissioner or any complainant may, in addition to any other right that that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the bank, authorized foreign bank, director, officer, employee or agent to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the bank, authorized foreign bank, director, officer, employee or agent from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the consumer provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

Subsection.Text: The definition biens immeubles in section 2 of the French version of the Cooperative Credit Associations Act is repealed.

Definition.Text: beneficial ownership芒聙聜includes ownership through one or more trustees, legal representatives, agents or other intermediaries;芒聙聜(v脙漏ritable propri脙漏taire et propri脙漏t脙漏 effective)
fiduciary芒聙聜means any person acting in a fiduciary capacity, and includes a personal representative of a deceased person;芒聙聜(repr脙漏sentant)
form of proxy芒聙聜means a written or printed form that, when completed and executed by or on behalf of a shareholder, constitutes a proxy;芒聙聜(formulaire de procuration)

personal representative芒聙聜means a person who stands in place of and represents another person and, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, includes, as the circumstances require, a trustee, an executor, an administrator, a committee, a guardian, a tutor, an assignee, a receiver, an agent or an attorney of any person, but does not include a delegate;芒聙聜(repr脙漏sentant personnel)

proxy芒聙聜means a completed and executed form of proxy by means of which a shareholder appoints a proxyholder to attend and act on the shareholder芒聙聶s behalf at a meeting of shareholders;芒聙聜(procuration)
proxyholder芒聙聜means the person appointed by proxy to attend and act on behalf of a shareholder at a meeting of shareholders;芒聙聜(fond脙漏 de pouvoir)
real property芒聙聜includes a leasehold interest in real property;芒聙聜(biens immeubles)

security interest芒聙聜means an interest in or charge on property by way of mortgage, lien, pledge or otherwise taken by a creditor or guarantor to secure the payment or performance of an obligation;芒聙聜(s脙禄ret脙漏)
trade, in respect of securities, means any sale or disposition of securities for valuable consideration;芒聙聜(op脙漏ration)

residential property芒聙聜means any real property or immovable consisting of buildings of which at least one half of the floor space is used, or is to be used, as one or more private dwellings;芒聙聜(immeuble r脙漏sidentiel)

Section.Text: Subsection 47(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A special resolution passed under subsection (1) is not valid unless prior written notice is given to the member setting forth the grounds on which the association is seeking to expel it and an opportunity is given to the member to appear, by an agent or mandatary or by a counsel, to make submissions at the meeting of the directors called to consider the resolution to expel it.

A special resolution passed under subsection (1) is not valid unless prior written notice is given to the member setting forth the grounds on which the association is seeking to expel it and an opportunity is given to the member to appear, by an agent or counsel, to make submissions at the meeting of the directors called to consider the resolution to expel it.

Section.Text: Section 51 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Lien, charge or right of retention
Section.Text: Unless the by-laws provide otherwise, if a member is indebted to an association, the membership shares and deposits recorded in the name of the member and any dividends payable to them are subject to a lien or, in Quebec, a charge or a right of retention in favour of the association to the extent of the debt.

Unless the by-laws otherwise provide, where a member is indebted to an association, the association has a lien to the extent of the debt on the membership shares and deposits recorded in the name of, and any dividends payable to, the member.
Paragraph 67(6)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Subsection 79(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An association may accept any membership share or share of the association surrendered to it as a gift, bequest or legacy, but must not extinguish or reduce a liability in respect of an amount unpaid on the surrendered share except in accordance with section 82.

An association may accept any membership share or share of the association surrendered to it as a gift, but may not extinguish or reduce a liability in respect of an amount unpaid on any such share except in accordance with section 82.

Section.Text: Section 101 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a minor exercises any rights of ownership in the securities of an association, no subsequent repudiation or avoidance or, in Quebec, annulment or reduction of the minor芒聙聶s obligations is effective against the association.
If a minor exercises any rights of ownership in the securities of an association, no subsequent repudiation or avoidance is effective against the association.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 103(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to the provisions of Part VIII and any applicable law relating to the collection of taxes, a person referred to in paragraph 100(2)(a) is entitled to become registered as the owner of a security, or to designate another person to be registered as the owner of a security, if the person referred to in paragraph 100(2)(a) delivers to the association or its transfer agent the following documents, accompanied by any assurance the association may require under section 134:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a grant of probate or of letters of administration or, in Quebec, the issuance of letters of verification or of a judgment probating a will, the original document or a copy certified to be a true copy by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the court that granted the probate or letters of administration or that issued the letters of verification or the judgment probating the will,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a trust company incorporated under the Trust and Loan Companies Act or under the laws of a province, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a lawyer or notary acting on behalf of the person referred to in paragraph 100(2)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a will probated by a notary in Quebec, a certified copy of the minutes of the probate;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of letters of verification issued by a notary in Quebec, a certified copy of the letters;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a notarial will in Quebec, an authentic copy of the will established in accordance with the laws of that province;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an affidavit or declaration of transmission made by the person referred to in paragraph 100(2)(a) that states the particulars of the transmission; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the security certificate that was owned by the deceased holder

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a transfer to the person referred to in paragraph 100(2)(a), with or without the endorsement of that person, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a transfer to any other person, endorsed in accordance with section 118.

Subsection.Text: Subject to the provisions of Part VIII and any applicable law relating to the collection of taxes, a person referred to in paragraph 100(2)(a) is entitled to become registered as the owner of a security, or to designate another person to be registered as the owner of a security, if the person referred to in paragraph 100(2)(a) delivers to the association or its transfer agent

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 103(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Despite subsection (1), if the laws of the jurisdiction governing the transmission of a security of a deceased holder do not require any of the documents mentioned in paragraphs (1)(a) to (c) in respect of the transmission, a personal representative of the deceased holder is entitled, subject to Part VIII and any applicable law relating to the collection of taxes, to become registered as the owner of the security or to designate a person to be registered as the owner, if the personal representative delivers to the association or its transfer agent the following documents:

Notwithstanding subsection (1), if the laws of the jurisdiction governing the transmission of a security of a deceased holder do not require a grant of probate or of letters of administration in respect of the transmission, a personal representative of the deceased holder is entitled, subject to Part VIII and any applicable law relating to the collection of taxes, to become registered as the owner or to designate a person to be registered as the owner, if the personal representative delivers to the association or its transfer agent the following documents, namely,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: reasonable proof of the governing laws, of the deceased holder芒聙聶s interest in the security and of the right of the personal representative or the designated person to become the registered shareholder.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 103(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: reasonable proof of the governing laws, of the deceased holder芒聙聶s interest or right in the security and of the right of the personal representative or the designated person to become the registered shareholder.

Section.Text: Subsection 107(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Even against a purchaser for value and without notice of a defect going to the validity of a security, the terms of the security include those stated on the security and those incorporated by reference to another document, instrument or act, statute, rule, regulation or order to the extent that the terms so referred to do not conflict with the stated terms, but the reference is not of itself notice to a purchaser for value of a defect going to the validity of the security, even if the security expressly states that a person accepting it admits the notice.

Even against a purchaser for value and without notice of a defect going to the validity of a security, the terms of the security include those stated on the security and those incorporated therein by reference to another instrument, statute, rule, regulation or order to the extent that the terms so referred to do not conflict with the stated terms, but such a reference is not of itself notice to a purchaser for value of a defect going to the validity of the security, notwithstanding that the security expressly states that a person accepting it admits the notice.

Section.Text: Subsection 111(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A purchaser acquires rights only to the extent of the interest or right purchased.
A purchaser of a limited interest acquires rights only to the extent of the interest purchased.

Section.Text: Subsection 115(5) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A securities broker gives to the broker芒聙聶s customer, to the issuer and to a purchaser, as the case may be, the warranties provided in subsections (1) to (4) and has the rights and privileges of a purchaser under those subsections, and those warranties of and in favour of the broker acting as an agent or mandatary are in addition to warranties given by the broker芒聙聶s customer and warranties given in favour of the broker芒聙聶s customer.

A securities broker gives to the broker芒聙聶s customer, to the issuer and to a purchaser, as the case may be, the warranties provided in subsections (1) to (4) and has the rights and privileges of a purchaser under those subsections, and those warranties of and in favour of the broker acting as an agent are in addition to warranties given by the broker芒聙聶s customer and warranties given in favour of the broker芒聙聶s customer.

Section.Text: Section 121 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Effect of failure by fiduciary to comply
Section.Text: Failure of a fiduciary to comply with a controlling instrument or act or with the law of the jurisdiction governing the fiduciary relationship, including any law requiring the fiduciary to obtain court approval of a transfer, does not render the fiduciary芒聙聶s endorsement unauthorized for the purposes of this Part.

Failure of a fiduciary to comply with a controlling instrument or with the law of the jurisdiction governing the fiduciary relationship, including any law requiring the fiduciary to obtain court approval of a transfer, does not render the fiduciary芒聙聶s endorsement unauthorized for the purposes of this Part.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 128(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

A transfer or pledge of a security shown in the records of a clearing agency or of an interest or right in such a security may, in addition to other methods, be effected by making an appropriate entry in those records if the security is evidenced by
If a security shown in the records of a clearing agency is evidenced by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a security certificate in the custody of the clearing agency or a custodian, or a nominee of either, subject to the instructions of the clearing agency, and is in bearer form or endorsed in blank by an appropriate person or registered in the name of the clearing agency or a custodian, or of a nominee of either, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an uncertificated security registered or recorded in records maintained by or on behalf of the association in the name of the clearing agency or a custodian, or of a nominee of either, subject to the instructions of the clearing agency,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: then, in addition to other methods, a transfer or pledge of the security or any interest therein may be effected by the making of an appropriate entry in the records of the clearing agency.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 128(4) of the English version of the Act after paragraph (b) is repealed.
Subsection 128(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Interests or rights in fungible bulk
Subsection.Text: Under subsections (4) to (10), entries may be in respect of similar securities or interests or rights in similar securities as part of a fungible bulk and may refer merely to a quantity of a particular security without reference to the name of the registered owner, certificate or bond number or any other information and, in appropriate cases, may be on a net basis taking into account other transfers or pledges of the same security.

Under subsections (4) to (10), entries may be in respect of like securities or interests therein as part of a fungible bulk and may refer merely to a quantity of a particular security without reference to the name of the registered owner, certificate or bond number or the like and, in appropriate cases, may be on a net basis taking into account other transfers or pledges of the same security.

A transfer or pledge under subsections (4) to (10) has the effect of a delivery of a security in bearer form or duly endorsed in blank representing the amount of the obligation or the number of shares or rights transferred or pledged.

If a pledge or the creation of a security interest is intended, the making of entries has the effect of a taking of delivery by the pledgee or a secured party and the pledgee or secured party shall be deemed to have taken possession for all purposes.
Subsections 128(6) and (7) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transfert ou gage 脙漏quivaut 脙聽 livraison
Subsection.Text: Le transfert ou le gage pr脙漏vu aux paragraphes (4) 脙聽 (10) 脙漏quivaut 脙聽 la livraison d芒聙聶une valeur mobili脙篓re au porteur ou d脙禄ment endoss脙漏e en blanc et repr脙漏sente soit le montant de l芒聙聶obligation, soit le nombre d芒聙聶actions ou de droits transf脙漏r脙漏s ou mis en gage.

Subsection.Text: Si le gage ou la cr脙漏ation d芒聙聶une s脙禄ret脙漏 est envisag脙漏, l芒聙聶inscription 脙漏quivaut 脙聽 une acceptation de la livraison par le cr脙漏ancier gagiste ou le cr脙漏ancier garanti et ces derniers sont r脙漏put脙漏s, 脙聽 toutes fins, en avoir pris possession.
Subsection 128(9) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Le transfert ou le gage effectu脙漏 en vertu des paragraphes (4) 脙聽 (10) ne constitue pas une inscription de transfert au sens des articles 133 脙聽 140.
A transfer or pledge under subsections (4) to (10) does not constitute a registration of transfer under sections 133 to 140.

Section.Text: Subsection 130(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: L芒聙聶acqu脙漏reur peut refuser le transfert ou en demander la r脙漏solution si le c脙漏dant ne se conforme pas, dans un d脙漏lai raisonnable, 脙聽 toute demande faite en vertu du paragraphe (1).
If a transferor fails to comply with a demand under subsection (1) within a reasonable time, the purchaser may reject or rescind the transfer.

Section.Text: Sections 131 and 132 of the Act are replaced by the following:
No seizure of a security or other interest or right evidenced by a security is effective until the person making the seizure obtains possession of the security.

Section.MarginalNote: No liability if good faith disposition
Section.Text: An agent or mandatary, or a bailee or depositary, who in good faith, including observance of reasonable commercial standards if they are in the business of buying, selling or otherwise dealing with securities of an association, has received securities and sold, pledged or delivered them according to the instructions of the principal or mandator, or the bailor or depositor, is not liable for conversion or for participation in breach of fiduciary duty even though the principal or mandator, or the bailor or depositor has no right to dispose of the securities.
No seizure of a security or other interest evidenced thereby is effective until the person making the seizure obtains possession of the security.

An agent or bailee who in good faith, including observance of reasonable commercial standards if the agent or bailee is in the business of buying, selling or otherwise dealing with securities of an association, has received securities and sold, pledged or delivered them according to the instructions of the agent芒聙聶s or bailee芒聙聶s principal is not liable for conversion or for participation in breach of fiduciary duty even though the principal has no right to dispose of the securities.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 166.02(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

A shareholder who is entitled to vote at a meeting of shareholders may, by executing or, in Quebec, signing a form of proxy, appoint a proxyholder or one or more alternate proxyholders, who are not required to be shareholders, to attend and act at the meeting in the manner and to the extent authorized by the proxy and with the authority conferred by the proxy.
A form of proxy is to be executed or, in Quebec, signed by a shareholder or by a shareholder芒聙聶s attorney authorized in writing to do so.

A shareholder who is entitled to vote at a meeting of shareholders may, by executing a form of proxy, appoint a proxyholder or one or more alternate proxyholders, who are not required to be shareholders, to attend and act at the meeting in the manner and to the extent authorized by the proxy and with the authority conferred by the proxy.
A form of proxy shall be executed by a shareholder or by a shareholder芒聙聶s attorney authorized in writing to do so.
Subsection 166.02(4) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A form of proxy must indicate, in boldface type, that the shareholder by whom or on whose behalf it is executed or, in Quebec, signed may appoint a proxyholder, other than a person designated in the form of proxy, to attend and act on the shareholder芒聙聶s behalf at a meeting to which the proxy relates, and must contain instructions as to the manner in which the shareholder may do so.

A form of proxy must indicate, in bold-face type, that the shareholder by whom or on whose behalf it is executed may appoint a proxyholder, other than a person designated in the form of proxy, to attend and act on the shareholder芒聙聶s behalf at a meeting to which the proxy relates, and must contain instructions as to the manner in which the shareholder may do so.
The portion of paragraph 166.02(6)(a) of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by depositing an instrument or act in writing executed or, in Quebec, signed by the shareholder or by the shareholder芒聙聶s attorney authorized in writing to do so

Subsection.Text: A shareholder may revoke a proxy

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: by depositing an instrument in writing executed by the shareholder or by the shareholder芒聙聶s attorney authorized in writing to do so

Section.Text: Section 166.03 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

The directors may specify, in a notice calling a meeting of shareholders or a continuation of a meeting of shareholders after an adjournment, a time before which proxies to be used at the meeting or the continued meeting must be deposited with the association or its transfer agent. The time specified must not be more than 48 hours, excluding Saturdays and holidays, before the meeting or the continued meeting.

The directors may specify, in a notice calling a meeting of shareholders or a continuation of a meeting of shareholders after an adjournment, a time before which executed forms of proxy to be used at the meeting or the continued meeting must be deposited with the association or its transfer agent. The time specified may not be more than forty-eight hours, excluding Saturdays and holidays, before the meeting or the continued meeting.
Subsection 166.06(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person who solicits a proxy and is appointed proxyholder must attend in person or cause an alternate proxyholder to attend every meeting in respect of which the proxy is valid, and the proxyholder or alternate proxyholder must comply with the directions of the shareholder who appointed them.

A person who solicits a proxy and is appointed proxyholder shall attend in person or cause an alternate proxyholder to attend every meeting in respect of which the proxy is valid, and the proxyholder or alternate proxyholder shall comply with the directions of the shareholder who executed the form of proxy.

Section.Text: Subsection 166.07(4) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Le sollicitant ou la personne agissant en son nom fournit dans les meilleurs d脙漏lais et 脙聽 ses propres frais 脙聽 l芒聙聶interm脙漏diaire, sur demande, le nombre n脙漏cessaire d芒聙聶exemplaires des documents vis脙漏s 脙聽 l芒聙聶alin脙漏a (1)a).

A person by or on behalf of whom a solicitation is made shall on request and without delay provide the intermediary, at the person芒聙聶s expense, with the necessary number of copies of the documents referred to in paragraph (1)(a).

Section.Text: Paragraph 170(h) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is an agent or mandatary or an employee of the government of a foreign country or any political subdivision of a foreign country.

Section.Text: The following persons are disqualified from being directors of an association:

Paragraph.Text: 脗聽

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is an agent or employee of the government of a foreign country or any political subdivision thereof.

Section.Text: Subsection 182(3) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: L芒聙聶association ou la personne agissant en son nom n芒聙聶engage pas sa responsabilit脙漏 en diffusant, conform脙漏ment au paragraphe (1), la d脙漏claration faite par un administrateur.
No association or person acting on its behalf incurs any liability by reason only of circulating a director芒聙聶s statement in compliance with subsection (1).

Section.Text: Paragraph 207(1)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: relates primarily to their remuneration as a director, an officer, an employee or an agent or mandatary of the association, an entity controlled by the association or an entity in which the association has a substantial investment;

Subsection.Text: A director who is required to make a disclosure under subsection 206(1) shall not be present at any meeting of directors, or of a committee of directors, while the contract or transaction is being considered or vote on any resolution to approve it unless the contract or transaction

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: relates primarily to their remuneration as a director, officer, employee or agent of the association, an entity controlled by the association or an entity in which the association has a substantial investment;

Section.Text: Subsection 214(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: When a director of an association pays a debt referred to in subsection (1) that is proven in liquidation and dissolution or winding-up proceedings, the director is entitled to any preference that the employee would have been entitled to and, if a judgment has been obtained, the director is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in Quebec, subrogated to the employee芒聙聶s rights as declared in the judgment; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: elsewhere in Canada, entitled to an assignment of the judgment.

Subsection.Text: Where a director of an association pays a debt referred to in subsection (1) that is proven in liquidation and dissolution or winding-up proceedings, the director is entitled to any preference that the employee would have been entitled to and, where a judgment has been obtained, the director is entitled to an assignment of the judgment.

Section.Text: Section 225 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Les modifications de l芒聙聶acte constitutif ou des r脙篓glements administratifs ne portent pas atteinte aux causes d芒聙聶actions ou aux r脙漏clamations d脙漏j脙聽 n脙漏es pouvant engager l芒聙聶association, ses administrateurs ou ses dirigeants, ni aux possibilit脙漏s de poursuite pour infraction, ni aux proc脙漏dures civiles, p脙漏nales ou administratives auxquelles ils sont parties.

No amendment to the incorporating instrument or by-laws of an association affects an existing cause of action or claim or liability to prosecution in favour of or against the association or its directors or officers, or any civil, criminal or administrative action or proceeding to which the association or any of its directors or officers are a party.
Subsection 232.1(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If an association or any director, any officer, any employee or any agent or mandatary of an association is contravening or has failed to comply with any term or condition made in respect of the issuance of letters patent of amalgamation, the Minister may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order directing the association or the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary to comply with the term or condition, cease the contravention or do any thing that is required to be done, and on the application the court may so order and make any other order it thinks fit.

If an association or any director, officer, employee or agent of an association is contravening or has failed to comply with any term or condition made in respect of the issuance of letters patent of amalgamation, the Minister may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order directing the association or the director, officer, employee or agent to comply with the term or condition, cease the contravention or do any thing that is required to be done, and on the application the court may so order and make any other order it thinks fit.

Section.Text: Paragraph 233(1)(d) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: d)
Paragraph.Text: aucune atteinte n芒聙聶est port脙漏e aux causes d芒聙聶actions ou aux r脙漏clamations d脙漏j脙聽 n脙漏es, ni aux possibilit脙漏s de poursuite pour infraction;

Subsection.Text: On the day provided for in the letters patent issued under section 232,

Paragraph.Text: 脗聽

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any existing cause of action, claim or liability to prosecution is unaffected;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 237(5) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person requiring an association to supply a basic list of members or shareholders may, if the person states in the accompanying affidavit that supplemental lists are required, request the association or its agent or mandatary, on payment of a reasonable fee, to provide supplemental lists of members or shareholders setting out any changes from the basic list in the names and addresses of the members or shareholders and the voting rights of each member or the number of shares owned by each shareholder for each business day following the date to which the basic list is made up.

A person requiring an association to supply a basic list of members or shareholders may, if the person states in the accompanying affidavit that supplemental lists are required, request the association or its agent, on payment of a reasonable fee, to provide supplemental lists of members or shareholders setting out any changes from the basic list in the names and addresses of the members or shareholders and the voting rights of each member or the number of shares owned by each shareholder for each business day following the date to which the basic list is made up.
The portion of subsection 237(6) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: When supplemental lists to be furnished
Subsection.Text: An association or its agent or mandatary must provide a supplemental list of members or shareholders required under subsection (5)
An association or its agent shall provide a supplemental list of members or shareholders required under subsection (5)

Section.Text: The portion of section 241 of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
An association and its agents or mandataries must take reasonable precautions to
An association and its agents shall take reasonable precautions to

Subsection.Text: Subsection 245(6) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person who wishes to examine the central securities register, take extracts from it or have copies of it made may, on payment of a reasonable fee, if they state in the accompanying affidavit that supplementary information is required, request the association or its agent or mandatary to provide supplementary information setting out any changes made to the register.

A person who wishes to examine a central securities register, take extracts from it or have copies of it made may on payment of a reasonable fee, if they state in the accompanying affidavit that supplementary information is required, request the association or its agent to provide supplementary information setting out any changes made to the register.
The portion of subsection 245(7) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: When supplementary information to be provided
Subsection.Text: An association or its agent or mandatary must provide the supplementary information within
An association or its agent shall provide the supplementary information within

Section.Text: Section 246 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
An association may appoint an agent or mandatary to maintain its central securities register.
An association may appoint an agent to maintain its central securities register.
The portion of section 249 of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
An association, its agent or mandatary or a trustee as defined in section 278 is not required to produce
An association, its agent or a trustee within the meaning of section 278 is not required to produce
Subsection 251(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A document executed or, in Quebec, signed on behalf of an association is not invalid merely because a corporate seal is not affixed to it.
A document executed on behalf of an association is not invalid merely because a corporate seal is not affixed to it.

Section.Text: The definitions option d芒聙聶achat and option de vente in subsection 260(1) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Est exclu de la pr脙漏sente d脙漏finition l芒聙聶option ou le droit d芒聙聶acqu脙漏rir des actions de la personne morale qui l芒聙聶accorde.芒聙聜(call)
option de vente芒聙聜Option n脙漏gociable par livraison qui permet de livrer un nombre pr脙漏cis d芒聙聶actions 脙聽 un prix et dans un d脙漏lai d脙漏termin脙漏s.芒聙聜(put)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 308(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

On the request of the auditor of an association, the present or former directors, officers, employees or agents or mandataries of the association must, to the extent that they are reasonably able to do so,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: permit access to any records, assets and security held by the association or any entity in which the association has a substantial investment that are, in the auditor芒聙聶s opinion, necessary to enable the auditor to perform their duties; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide any information and explanations that are, in the auditor芒聙聶s opinion, necessary to enable the auditor to perform their duties.

Subsection.Text: On the request of the auditor of an association, the present or former directors, officers, employees or agents of the association shall, to the extent that such persons are reasonably able to do so,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: permit access to such records, assets and security held by the association or any entity in which the association has a substantial investment, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide such information and explanations

Section.Text: Subsection 320(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An application made or an action brought or intervened in under subsection 318(1) or section 322 must not be stayed, discontinued, settled or dismissed for want of prosecution or, in Quebec, failure to act within a time limit set under the applicable rules of civil procedure, without the approval of the court given on any terms that the court thinks fit and, if the court determines that the interests or rights of any complainant might be substantially affected by any stay, discontinuance, settlement or dismissal, the court may order any party to the application or action to give notice to the complainant.

An application made or an action brought or intervened in under subsection 318(1) or section 322 shall not be stayed, discontinued, settled or dismissed for want of prosecution without the approval of the court given on such terms as the court thinks fit and, if the court determines that the interests of any complainant might be substantially affected by any stay, discontinuance, settlement or dismissal, the court may order any party to the application or action to give notice to the complainant.

Section.Text: Paragraph 333(b) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: b)

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order appointing a liquidator, with or without security, fixing a liquidator芒聙聶s remuneration and replacing a liquidator;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: do all acts and execute or, in Quebec, sign documents in the name and on behalf of the association;

Subsection.Text: A liquidator may

Paragraph.Text: 脗聽

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: do all acts and execute documents in the name and on behalf of the association;

Section.Text: Subsection 341(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A liquidator must give notice of their intention to make an application under subsection (1) to the Superintendent, to each inspector appointed under section 333, to each member or shareholder of the association and to any person who provided a security or a fidelity bond or fidelity insurance for the liquidation.

A liquidator shall give notice of the liquidator芒聙聶s intention to make an application under subsection (1) to the Superintendent, to each inspector appointed under section 333, to each member or shareholder of the association and to any person who provided a security or fidelity bond for the liquidation.

Section.Text: Paragraph 347(4)(a) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: de joindre comme partie 脙聽 l芒聙聶instance chaque ancien associ脙漏 ou actionnaire retrouv脙漏 par le demandeur;

Subsection.Text: If the plaintiff establishes a claim in an action under subsection (3), the court may refer the proceedings to a referee or other officer of the court who may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: add as a party to the proceedings each person found by the plaintiff to have been a member or shareholder;

Section.Text: Section 372 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

An association and any person who is a director, an officer, an employee or an agent or mandatary of the association may rely on any information contained in a declaration required by the directors under section 371 or on any information otherwise acquired in respect of any matter that might be the subject of a declaration under that section, and no action lies against the association or the person for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in reliance on the information.

An association and any person who is a director or an officer, employee or agent of the association may rely on any information contained in a declaration required by the directors pursuant to section 371 or on any information otherwise acquired in respect of any matter that might be the subject of such a declaration, and no action lies against the association or any such person for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in reliance on any such information.
Paragraph 376(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: hold, manage and otherwise deal with real property or immovables;

Subsection.Text: In addition, an association may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: hold, manage and otherwise deal with real property;

Section.Text: Paragraph 377(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: act as agent or mandatary for any entity referred to in paragraph 375(1)(a), any member of a cooperative credit society or, if the association is a retail association, any other person in respect of the provision of any service that is provided by a financial institution, a permitted entity as defined in subsection 386(1) or a prescribed entity and enter into an arrangement with any person in respect of the provision of that service; or

Section.Text: Subject to section 381, an association may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: act as agent for any entity referred to in paragraph 375(1)(a), any member of a cooperative credit society or, if the association is a retail association, any other person in respect of the provision of any service that is provided by a financial institution, a permitted entity as defined in subsection 386(1) or a prescribed entity and may enter into an arrangement with any person in respect of the provision of that service; or

Section.Text: Section 378 of the Act is replaced by the following:
It is prohibited for an association to act in Canada

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as an executor or administrator of an estate or liquidator of a succession;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: as a trustee for a trust;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: as a guardian or tutor or committee of an incapable person; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in any capacity similar to the capacities mentioned in paragraph (c).

Section.Text: No association shall act in Canada as

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an executor, administrator or official guardian or a guardian, tutor, curator, judicial adviser or committee of a mentally incompetent person; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a trustee for a trust.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 378.3(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to the regulations, a retail association referred to in paragraph 378.1(1)(b) or (c) must not, in respect of its business in Canada, act as agent or mandatary for any person in the taking of a deposit that is less than $150,000 and payable in Canada.

Subject to the regulations, a retail association referred to in paragraph 378.1(1)(b) or (c) may not, in respect of its business in Canada, act as agent for any person in the taking of a deposit that is less than $150,000 and payable in Canada.
Subsection 378.3(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the circumstances in which, and the conditions under which, a retail association referred to in subsection (1) may act as agent or mandatary for any person in the taking of a deposit that is less than $150,000 and payable in Canada.

The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the circumstances in which, and the conditions under which, a retail association referred to in subsection (1) may act as agent for any person in the taking of a deposit that is less than $150,000 and payable in Canada.

Section.Text: Section 382 of the Act is replaced by the following:
An association must not engage in Canada in any personal property or movable leasing activity in which a financial leasing entity, as defined in subsection 386(1), is not permitted to engage.
An association shall not engage in Canada in any personal property leasing activity in which a financial leasing entity, within the meaning of subsection 386(1), is not permitted to engage.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 382.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A retail association must not make a loan in Canada on the security of residential property in Canada for the purpose of purchasing, renovating or improving that residential property, or refinance a loan for that purpose, if the amount of the loan, together with the amount outstanding of any mortgage or hypothec of equal or higher rank on the residential property, would exceed 80% of the value of the residential property at the time of the loan.

A retail association shall not make a loan in Canada on the security of residential property in Canada for the purpose of purchasing, renovating or improving that property, or refinance such a loan, if the amount of the loan, together with the amount then outstanding of any mortgage having an equal or prior claim against the property, would exceed 80 per cent of the value of the property at the time of the loan.
Paragraph 382.1(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made or guaranteed under the National Housing Act or any other Act of Parliament by or under which a different limit on the value of residential property on the security of which the association may make a loan is established;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made or guaranteed under the National Housing Act or any other Act of Parliament by or under which a different limit on the value of property on the security of which the association may make a loan is established;
脗聽

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a loan secured by a mortgage where

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the mortgage is taken back by the association on a property disposed of by the association, including where the disposition is by way of a realization of a security interest, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the mortgage secures payment of an amount payable to the association for the property.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 382.1(2)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a loan secured by a mortgage or hypothec if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the mortgage or hypothec is taken back by the association on a property disposed of by the association, including when the disposition is by way of a realization of a security interest, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the mortgage or hypothec secures payment of an amount payable to the association for the property.

Section.Text: Section 384 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
An association must not grant to a person the right to appoint a receiver, receiver and manager or sequestrator of the property or business of the association.
An association shall not grant to a person the right to appoint a receiver or a receiver and manager of the property or business of the association.
Subsection 385.02(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Le paragraphe (1) s芒聙聶applique que la fiducie soit expresse ou par effet de la loi et s芒聙聶applique m脙陋me si l芒聙聶association en a 脙漏t脙漏 avis脙漏e si elle agit sur l芒聙聶ordre ou sous l芒聙聶autorit脙漏 du ou des titulaires du compte dans lequel le d脙漏p脙麓t est effectu脙漏.

Subsection (1) applies regardless of whether the trust is express or arises by the operation of law, and it applies even when the association has notice of the trust if it acts on the order of or under the authority of the holder or holders of the account into which the deposit is made.

Section.Text: Subsection 385.15(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a retail association makes a loan in respect of which the disclosure requirements of section 385.16 apply, and the loan is not secured by a mortgage on real property or a hypothec on an immovable and is required to be repaid either on a fixed future date or by instalments, the association must, if there is a prepayment of the loan, rebate to the borrower a portion of the charges included in the cost of borrowing in respect of the loan.

If a retail association makes a loan in respect of which the disclosure requirements of section 385.16 apply and the loan is not secured by a mortgage on real property and is required to be repaid either on a fixed future date or by instalments, the association shall, if there is a prepayment of the loan, rebate to the borrower a portion of the charges included in the cost of borrowing in respect of the loan.

Section.Text: Section 385.19 of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a retail association makes a loan in respect of which the disclosure requirements of section 385.16 apply and the loan is secured by a mortgage on real property or a hypothec on an immovable the association must disclose to the borrower, at the prescribed time and place and in the prescribed form and manner, any information that is prescribed respecting the renewal of the loan.

If a retail association makes a loan in respect of which the disclosure requirements of section 385.16 apply and the loan is secured by a mortgage on real property, the association shall disclose to the borrower, at the prescribed time and place and in the prescribed form and manner, any information that is prescribed respecting the renewal of the loan.
Paragraph 385.25(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: secured by a mortgage on real property or a hypothec on an immovable; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of a loan that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: secured by a mortgage on real property; or

Section.Text: Subsection 385.3(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If the transmission of a debt owing by a retail association by reason of a deposit, of property held by a retail association as security or for safekeeping or of rights with respect to a safety deposit box and property deposited in it takes place because of the death of a person, the delivery to the association of the following is sufficient justification and authority for giving effect to the transmission:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an affidavit or declaration in writing that is in a form satisfactory to the association, that is signed by or on behalf of a person claiming by virtue of the transmission and that states the nature and effect of the transmission; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: one of the following documents:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the claim is based on a will or other testamentary instrument, on a grant of probate of a will or other testamentary instrument with or without letters testamentary or other document of similar import, on a grant of letters of administration or other document of similar import, on a judgment probating a will or other document of similar import, on letters of verification or other document of similar import, or on a certified copy of the notarial minutes of probate of a will or other document of similar import, purporting to be issued by any court or authority in Canada or elsewhere, an authenticated copy or certificate of the document under the seal of the court or authority without proof of the authenticity of the seal or other proof, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the claim is based on a notarial will, an authentic copy of the notarial will.

Subsection.Text: If the transmission of a debt owing by a retail association by reason of a deposit, of property held by a retail association as security or for safe-keeping or of rights with respect to a safety deposit box and property deposited therein takes place because of the death of a person, the delivery to the association of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an affidavit or declaration in writing in a form satisfactory to the association signed by or on behalf of a person claiming by virtue of the transmission stating the nature and effect of the transmission, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: one of the following documents, namely,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: when the claim is based on a will or other testamentary instrument or on a grant of probate thereof or on such a grant and letters testamentary or other document of like import or on a grant of letters of administration or other document of like import, purporting to be issued by any court of authority in Canada or elsewhere, an authenticated copy or certificate thereof under the seal of the court or authority without proof of the authenticity of the seal or other proof, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: when the claim is based on a notarial will, an authenticated copy thereof,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: is sufficient justification and authority for giving effect to the transmission in accordance with the claim.

Section.Text: Section 401 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

An association must not, and must not permit its prescribed subsidiaries to, purchase or otherwise acquire an interest in real property or make an improvement to any real property or immovable in respect of which the association or any of its prescribed subsidiaries has an interest in real property if the aggregate value of all interests of the association in real property exceeds, or the acquisition of the interest or the making of the improvement would cause that aggregate value to exceed, the prescribed percentage of the regulatory capital of the association.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 232:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of the heading and section 401:

Section.Text: An association shall not, and shall not permit its prescribed subsidiaries to, purchase or otherwise acquire an interest in real property or make an improvement to any real property in which the association or any of its prescribed subsidiaries has an interest if the aggregate value of all interests of the association in real property exceeds, or the acquisition of the interest or the making of the improvement would cause that aggregate value to exceed, the prescribed percentage of the regulatory capital of the association.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 412(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Part, loan includes a deposit, a financial lease, a conditional sale contract, an instalment sale contract, a repurchase agreement and any other similar arrangement for obtaining funds or credit, but does not include investments in securities or the making of an acceptance, endorsement or other guarantee.

For the purposes of this Part, loan includes a deposit, a financial lease, a conditional sales contract, a repurchase agreement and any other similar arrangement for obtaining funds or credit, but does not include investments in securities or the making of an acceptance, endorsement or other guarantee.

For the purposes of this Part, security of a related party includes an option, transferable by delivery, to demand delivery of a specified number or amount of shares of the related party at a fixed price within a specified time.

Section.Text: An association may make a loan to or a guarantee on behalf of a related party of the association or take an assignment of or otherwise acquire a loan to a related party of the association if

Paragraph.Text: 脗聽

Subsection.Text: Subsection 418(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite any of the provisions of subsections (1) and (2), an association may, in the normal course of business and in accordance with arrangements that have been approved by the Superintendent in writing, acquire or dispose of any assets, other than real property or immovables, from or to a related party of the association that is a financial institution.

Notwithstanding any of the provisions of subsections (1) and (2), an association may, in the normal course of business and pursuant to arrangements that have been approved by the Superintendent in writing, acquire or dispose of any assets, other than real property, from or to a related party of the association that is a financial institution.

Despite any of the provisions of subsections (1) and (2), an association may acquire any assets from, or dispose of any assets to, a related party of the association as part of, or in the course of, a restructuring, if the acquisition or disposition has been approved in writing by the Superintendent.
Subsection 418(3.1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Op脙漏rations dans le cadre d芒聙聶une restructuration
Subsection.Text: Par d脙漏rogation aux paragraphes (1) et (2), dans le cadre d芒聙聶une restructuration, l芒聙聶association peut, avec l芒聙聶agr脙漏ment 脙漏crit du surintendant, acqu脙漏rir des 脙漏l脙漏ments d芒聙聶actif d芒聙聶un apparent脙漏 ou en disposer en sa faveur.

L芒聙聶association peut acqu脙漏rir des 脙漏l脙漏ments d芒聙聶actif d芒聙聶un apparent脙漏 ou en disposer en sa faveur dans le cadre d芒聙聶une convention de vente approuv脙漏e par le ministre en vertu de l芒聙聶article 233.5.
An association may acquire any assets from, or dispose of any assets to, a related party of the association under a sale agreement that is approved by the Minister under section 233.5.

Section.Text: Subsection 428(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An association and any person who is a director, an officer, an employee or an agent or mandatary of the association may rely on any information contained in any disclosure received by the association under subsection (1) or any information otherwise acquired in respect of any matter that might be the subject of a disclosure under subsection (1), and no action lies against the association or the person for anything done or omitted in good faith in reliance on the information.

An association and any person who is a director or an officer, employee or agent of the association may rely on any information contained in any disclosure received by the association pursuant to subsection (1) or any information otherwise acquired in respect of any matter that might be the subject of such a disclosure and no action lies against the association or any such person for anything done or omitted in good faith in reliance on any such information.
