Section.Text: Section 467 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an entity commits an offence under this Act, any director, any officer or any agent or mandatary of the entity who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to and guilty of the offence and liable on summary conviction or on conviction on indictment to the punishment provided under paragraph 466(1)(a) for the offence, whether or not the entity has been prosecuted or convicted.

Where an entity commits an offence under this Act, any officer, director or agent of the entity who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to and guilty of the offence and liable on summary conviction or on conviction on indictment to the punishment provided under paragraph 466(1)(a) for the offence, whether or not the entity has been prosecuted or convicted.
Section 469 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If an association or any director, any officer, any employee or any agent or mandatary of an association does not comply with any provision of this Act or the regulations other than a consumer provision, or of the incorporating instrument or any by-law of the association, the Superintendent, any complainant or any creditor of the association may, in addition to any other right that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the association, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the association, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Compliance or restraining order 芒聙聰 consumer provisions
Subsection.Text: If a retail association or any director, any officer, any employee or any agent or mandatary of a retail association does not comply with any applicable consumer provision, the Commissioner or any complainant may, in addition to any other right that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the retail association, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the retail association, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the consumer provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

If an association or any director, officer, employee or agent of an association does not comply with any provision of this Act or the regulations other than a consumer provision, or of the incorporating instrument or any by-law of the association, the Superintendent, any complainant or any creditor of the association may, in addition to any other right that that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the association, director, officer, employee or agent to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the association, director, officer, employee or agent from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

If a retail association or any director, officer, employee or agent of a retail association does not comply with any applicable consumer provision, the Commissioner or any complainant may, in addition to any other right that that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the retail association, director, officer, employee or agent to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the retail association, director, officer, employee or agent from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the consumer provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.
The definition biens immeubles in subsection 2(1) of the French version of the Insurance Companies Act is repealed.

Definition.Text: beneficial ownership芒聙聜includes ownership through one or more trustees, legal representatives, agents or other intermediaries;芒聙聜(v脙漏ritable propri脙漏taire et propri脙漏t脙漏 effective)
fiduciary芒聙聜means any person acting in a fiduciary capacity and includes a personal representative of a deceased person;芒聙聜(repr脙漏sentant)

form of proxy芒聙聜means a written or printed form that, when completed and executed by or on behalf of a shareholder or a policyholder, or, in the case of a fraternal benefit society, a member, constitutes a proxy;芒聙聜(formulaire de procuration)

personal representative芒聙聜means a person who stands in place of and represents another person and, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, includes, as the circumstances require, a trustee, an executor, an administrator, a committee, a guardian, a tutor, an assignee, a receiver, an agent or an attorney of any person;芒聙聜(repr脙漏sentant personnel)

proxy芒聙聜means a completed and executed form of proxy by means of which a shareholder or policyholder or, in the case of a society, a member, appoints a proxyholder to attend and act on behalf of the shareholder, policyholder or member at a meeting of shareholders, policyholders or members;芒聙聜(procuration)

proxyholder芒聙聜means the person appointed by proxy to attend and act on behalf of a shareholder or a policyholder or, in the case of a society, a member at a meeting of shareholders, policyholders or members;芒聙聜(fond脙漏 de pouvoir)
real property芒聙聜includes a leasehold interest in real property;芒聙聜(biens immeubles)

securities underwriter芒聙聜means a person who, as principal, agrees to purchase securities with a view to the distribution of the securities or who, as agent for a body corporate or other person, offers for sale or sells securities in connection with a distribution of the securities, and includes a person who participates, directly or indirectly, in a distribution of securities, other than a person whose interest in the distribution of securities is limited to receiving a distributor芒聙聶s or seller芒聙聶s commission payable by a securities underwriter;芒聙聜(souscripteur 脙聽 forfait)

security interest芒聙聜means an interest in or charge on property by way of mortgage, lien, pledge or otherwise taken by a creditor or guarantor to secure the payment or performance of an obligation;芒聙聜(s脙禄ret脙漏)
trade, in respect of securities, means any sale or disposition of securities for valuable consideration;芒聙聜(op脙漏ration)

Subsection.Text: The definitions fiduciary, personal representative, proxy, residential property and security interest in subsection 2(1) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: fiduciary芒聙聜means any person who is acting in a fiduciary capacity or who has an obligation of honesty and loyalty to another person because they are in a relationship of trust and confidence with that person, and includes a personal representative of a deceased person;芒聙聜(repr脙漏sentant)

Section.Text: Subsection 104(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Even against a purchaser for value and without notice of a defect going to the validity of a security, the terms of the security include those stated on the security and those incorporated by reference to another document, instrument or act, statute, rule, regulation or order to the extent that the terms so referred to do not conflict with the stated terms, but the reference is not of itself notice to a purchaser for value of a defect going to the validity of the security, even if the security expressly states that a person accepting it admits the notice.

Even against a purchaser for value and without notice of a defect going to the validity of a security, the terms of the security include those stated on the security and those incorporated therein by reference to another instrument, statute, rule, regulation or order to the extent that the terms so referred to do not conflict with the stated terms, but such a reference is not of itself notice to a purchaser for value of a defect going to the validity of the security, notwithstanding that the security expressly states that a person accepting it admits the notice.

A securities broker gives to the broker芒聙聶s customer, to the issuer and to a purchaser, as the case may be, the warranties provided in subsections脗聽(1) to (4) and has the rights and privileges of a purchaser under those subsections, and those warranties of and in favour of the broker acting as an agent are in addition to warranties given by the broker芒聙聶s customer and warranties given in favour of the broker芒聙聶s customer.

Section.Text: Failure of a fiduciary to comply with a controlling instrument or act or with the law of the jurisdiction governing the fiduciary relationship, including any law requiring the fiduciary to obtain court approval of a transfer, does not render the fiduciary芒聙聶s endorsement unauthorized for the purposes of this Part.

Failure of a fiduciary to comply with a controlling instrument or with the law of the jurisdiction governing the fiduciary relationship, including any law requiring the fiduciary to obtain court approval of a transfer, does not render the fiduciary芒聙聶s endorsement unauthorized for the purposes of this Part.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 125(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

A transfer or pledge of a security shown in the records of a clearing agency or of an interest or right in such a security may, in addition to other methods, be effected by making an appropriate entry in those records if the security is evidenced by
If a security shown in the records of a clearing agency is evidenced by

Paragraph.Text: a security certificate in the custody of the clearing agency or a custodian, or a nominee of either, subject to the instructions of the clearing agency, and is in bearer form or endorsed in blank by an appropriate person or registered in the name of the clearing agency or a custodian, or of a nominee of either, or

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 125(4) of the English version of the Act after paragraph (b) is repealed.
Subsection 125(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Interests or rights in fungible bulk
Subsection.Text: Under subsections (4) to (10), entries may be in respect of similar securities or interests or rights in similar securities as part of a fungible bulk and may refer merely to a quantity of a particular security without reference to the name of the registered owner, certificate or bond number or any other information and, in appropriate cases, may be on a net basis taking into account other transfers or pledges of the same security.

Under subsections脗聽(4) to (10), entries may be in respect of like securities or interests therein as part of a fungible bulk and may refer merely to a quantity of a particular security without reference to the name of the registered owner, certificate or bond number or the like and, in appropriate cases, may be on a net basis taking into account other transfers or pledges of the same security.

A transfer or pledge under subsections脗聽(4) to (10) has the effect of a delivery of a security in bearer form or duly endorsed in blank representing the amount of the obligation or the number of shares or rights transferred or pledged.

If a pledge or the creation of a security interest is intended, the making of entries has the effect of a taking of delivery by the pledgee or a secured party and the pledgee or secured party shall be deemed to have taken possession for all purposes.

A transfer or pledge under subsections脗聽(4) to (10) does not constitute a registration of transfer under sections脗聽130 to 137.

No seizure of a security or other interest or right evidenced by a security is effective until the person making the seizure obtains possession of the security.

Section.MarginalNote: No liability if good faith disposition
Section.Text: An agent or mandatary, or a bailee or depositary, who in good faith, including observance of reasonable commercial standards if they are in the business of buying, selling or otherwise dealing with securities of a company, has received securities and sold, pledged or delivered them according to the instructions of the principal or mandator, or the bailor or depositor, is not liable for conversion or for participation in breach of fiduciary duty even though the principal or mandator, or the bailor or depositor has no right to dispose of the securities.
No seizure of a security or other interest evidenced thereby is effective until the person making the seizure obtains possession of the security.

An agent or bailee who in good faith, including observance of reasonable commercial standards if the agent or bailee is in the business of buying, selling or otherwise dealing with securities of a company, has received securities and sold, pledged or delivered them according to the instructions of the agent芒聙聶s or bailee芒聙聶s principal is not liable for conversion or for participation in breach of fiduciary duty even though the principal has no right to dispose of the securities.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 164.02(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Section 244 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Les modifications de l芒聙聶acte constitutif ou des r脙篓glements administratifs ne portent pas atteinte aux causes d芒聙聶actions ou aux r脙漏clamations d脙漏j脙聽 n脙漏es pouvant engager la soci脙漏t脙漏, ses administrateurs ou ses dirigeants, ni aux possibilit脙漏s de poursuite pour infraction, ni aux proc脙漏dures civiles, p脙漏nales ou administratives auxquelles ils sont parties.

Subsection.Text: If a company or society, or any director, any officer, any employee or any agent or mandatary of a company or society, is contravening or has failed to comply with any term or condition made in respect of the issuance of letters patent of amalgamation, the Minister may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order directing the company or society, or the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary, to comply with the term or condition, cease the contravention or do any thing that is required to be done, and on the application the court may so order and make any other order it thinks fit.

If a company or society, or any director, officer, employee or agent of a company or society, is contravening or has failed to comply with any term or condition made in respect of the issuance of letters patent of amalgamation, the Minister may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order directing the company or society, or the director, officer, employee or agent, to comply with the term or condition, cease the contravention or do any thing that is required to be done, and on the application the court may so order and make any other order it thinks fit.

Subsection.Text: The portion of the definition agent in section 406.1 of the English version of the Act before subparagraph (a)(ii) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Subparagraph.Label: (i)

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)

Section.Text: Section 436 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A company and any person who is a director, an officer, an employee or an agent or mandatary of the company may rely on any information contained in a declaration required by the directors under section 435 or on any information otherwise acquired in respect of any matter that might be the subject of a declaration under section 435, and no action lies against the company or the person for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in reliance on the information.

A company and any person who is a director or an officer, employee or agent of the company may rely on any information contained in a declaration required by the directors pursuant to section 435 or on any information otherwise acquired in respect of any matter that might be the subject of such a declaration, and no action lies against the company or any such person for anything done or omitted to be done in good faith in reliance on any such information.

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: hold, manage and otherwise deal with real property or immovables;

Subsection.Text: In addition, a company may

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Paragraph.Label: (b)

Section.Text: Section 466 of the Act is replaced by the following:
It is prohibited for a company to act in Canada

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: as an executor or administrator of an estate or liquidator of a succession;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: as a trustee for a trust;

Paragraph.Label: (c)

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in any capacity similar to the capacities mentioned in paragraph (c).

Section.Text: No company shall act in Canada as

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an executor, administrator or official guardian or a guardian, tutor, curator, judicial adviser or committee of a mentally incompetent person; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a trustee for a trust.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 469(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A company must not make a loan in Canada on the security of residential property in Canada for the purpose of purchasing, renovating or improving that residential property, or refinance a loan for that purpose, if the amount of the loan, together with the amount outstanding of any mortgage or hypothec of equal or higher rank on the residential property, would exceed 80% of the value of the residential property at the time of the loan.

A company shall not make a loan in Canada on the security of residential property in Canada for the purpose of purchasing, renovating or improving that property, or refinance such a loan, if the amount of the loan, together with the amount then outstanding of any mortgage having an equal or prior claim against the property, would exceed 80 per cent of the value of the property at the time of the loan.

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made or guaranteed under the National Housing Act or any other Act of Parliament by or under which a different limit on the value of residential property on the security of which the company may make a loan is established;

Subsection.Text: Subsection脗聽(1) does not apply in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a loan made or guaranteed under the National Housing Act or any other Act of Parliament by or pursuant to which a different limit on the value of property on the security of which the company may make a loan is established;

Section.Text: Section 471 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
A company must not grant to a person the right to appoint a receiver, receiver and manager or sequestrator of the property or business of the company.

A property and casualty company, or a marine company, must not engage in Canada in any financial leasing of personal property or movables.
A property and casualty company, or a marine company, shall not engage in Canada in any financial leasing of personal property.

Where a company makes a loan in respect of which the disclosure requirements of section脗聽480 apply and the loan is not secured by a mortgage on real property and is required to be repaid either on a fixed future date or by instalments, the company shall, if there is a prepayment of the loan, rebate to the borrower a portion of the charges included in the cost of borrowing in respect of the loan.

A member who is entitled to vote by by-law of a society may, if the by-laws of the society so provide, by executing an instrument of proxy, appoint a proxyholder or one or more alternate proxyholders, who are not required to be members of the society, to attend and act at the meeting in the manner and to the extent authorized by the proxy and with the authority conferred by the proxy.
An instrument of proxy is not valid at a meeting of a society unless it is filed with the secretary of the society at least ten days before the date of the meeting and it may be revoked at any time.

Section.Text: Section 618 of the Act and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Limit 芒聙聰 foreign life companies

Subsection.Text: The total accepted value of interests in real property or rights in immovables vested in trust by a foreign life company for the classes of life insurance, accident and sickness insurance, credit protection insurance and other approved products insurance must not at any time exceed the prescribed percentage of the value of its assets in Canada for those classes.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Limit 芒聙聰 foreign composite companies
Subsection.Text: The total accepted value of interests in real property or rights in immovables vested in trust by a foreign life company for the classes of insurance other than life insurance, accident and sickness insurance, credit protection insurance and other approved products insurance must not at any time exceed the prescribed percentage of the value of its assets in Canada for those classes.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Limit 芒聙聰 foreign property and casualty companies and foreign marine companies
Subsection.Text: The total accepted value of interests in real property or rights in immovables vested in trust by a foreign property and casualty company, or a foreign marine company, must not at any time exceed the prescribed percentage of the value of its assets in Canada.

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 343:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Existing text of the heading and section 618:

Subsection.Text: The total accepted value of interests in real property vested in trust by a foreign life company for the classes of life insurance, accident and sickness insurance, credit protection insurance and other approved products insurance shall not at any time exceed the prescribed percentage of the value of its assets in Canada for those classes.

The total accepted value of interests in real property vested in trust by a foreign life company for the classes of insurance other than life insurance, accident and sickness insurance, credit protection insurance and other approved products insurance, shall not at any time exceed the prescribed percentage of the value of its assets in Canada for those classes.

The total accepted value of interests in real property vested in trust by a foreign property and casualty company, or a foreign marine company, shall not at any time exceed the prescribed percentage of the value of its assets in Canada.

Section.Text: Section 787 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The directors may specify, in a notice calling a meeting of shareholders or a continuation of a meeting of shareholders after an adjournment, a time before which proxies to be used at the meeting or the continued meeting must be deposited with the insurance holding company or its transfer agent.
The time specified for the deposit of proxies must not precede the meeting or the continued meeting by more than 48 hours, excluding Saturdays and holidays.

Section.Text: Section 856 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Les modifications de l芒聙聶acte constitutif ou des r脙篓glements administratifs ne portent pas atteinte aux causes d芒聙聶actions ou aux r脙漏clamations d脙漏j脙聽 n脙漏es pouvant engager la soci脙漏t脙漏 de portefeuille d芒聙聶assurances, ses administrateurs ou ses dirigeants, ni aux possibilit脙漏s de poursuite pour infraction, ni aux proc脙漏dures civiles, p脙漏nales ou administratives auxquelles ils sont parties.

No amendment to the incorporating instrument or by-laws of an insurance holding company affects an existing cause of action or claim or liability to prosecution in favour of or against the insurance holding company or its directors or officers, or any civil, criminal or administrative action or proceeding to which the insurance holding company or any of its directors or officers are a party.

If an insurance holding company, or any director, officer, employee or agent of an insurance holding company, is contravening or has failed to comply with any term or condition made in respect of the issuance of letters patent of amalgamation, the Minister may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order directing the insurance holding company, or the director, officer, employee or agent to comply with the term or condition, cease the contravention or do any thing that is required to be done, and on the application the court may so order and make any other order it thinks fit.

Section.Text: Section 1028 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

If an entity commits an offence under this Act, any director, any officer or any agent or mandatary of the entity who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to and guilty of the offence and liable on summary conviction or on conviction on indictment to the punishment provided under paragraph脗聽1027(1)(a) for the offence, whether or not the entity has been prosecuted or convicted.

If an entity commits an offence under this Act, any officer, director or agent of the entity who directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of the offence is a party to and guilty of the offence and liable on summary conviction or on conviction on indictment to the punishment provided under paragraph脗聽1027(1)(a) for the offence, whether or not the entity has been prosecuted or convicted.

Subsection.Text: If a company, a society, a foreign company, a provincial company or an insurance holding company or any director, any officer, any employee or any agent or mandatary of one does not comply with any provision of this Act or the regulations other than a consumer provision, or, in the case of a company, a society or an insurance holding company, of the incorporating instrument or any by-law of the company, society or insurance holding company, the Superintendent, any complainant or any creditor of the company, society or insurance holding company may, in addition to any other right that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the company, the society, the foreign company, the provincial company, the insurance holding company, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the company, the society, the foreign company, the provincial company, the insurance holding company, the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

If a company, a society, a foreign company, a provincial company or an insurance holding company or any director, officer, employee or agent of one does not comply with any provision of this Act or the regulations other than a consumer provision, or, in the case of a company, a society or an insurance holding company, of the incorporating instrument or any by-law of the company, society or insurance holding company, the Superintendent, any complainant or any creditor of the company, society or insurance holding company may, in addition to any other right that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the company, society, foreign company, provincial company, insurance holding company, director, officer, employee or agent to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the company, society, foreign company, provincial company, insurance holding company, director, officer, employee or agent from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

If a company or a foreign company or any director, officer, employee or agent of one does not comply with any applicable consumer provision, the Commissioner or any complainant may, in addition to any other right that person has, apply to a court for an order directing the company, foreign company, director, officer, employee or agent to comply with 芒聙聰 or restraining the company, foreign company, director, officer, employee or agent from acting in breach of 芒聙聰 the consumer provision and, on the application, the court may so order and make any further order it thinks fit.

Definition.Text: beneficial ownership芒聙聜includes ownership through one or more trustees, legal representatives, agents or other intermediaries;芒聙聜(v脙漏ritable propri脙漏taire et propri脙漏t脙漏 effective)
fiduciary芒聙聜means any person acting in a fiduciary capacity and includes a personal representative of a deceased person;芒聙聜(repr脙漏sentant)
form of proxy芒聙聜means a written or printed form that, when completed and executed by or on behalf of a shareholder, constitutes a proxy;芒聙聜(formulaire de procuration)

guaranteed trust money芒聙聜means money that is received in trust for investment by a company that is a trust company pursuant to subsection 57(2) and that is subject to a guarantee by the company in respect of the payment of interest or repayment of the principal or both;芒聙聜(fonds en fiducie garantie)

personal representative芒聙聜means a person who stands in place of and represents another person and, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, includes, as the circumstances require, a trustee, an executor, an administrator, a committee, a guardian, a tutor, an assignee, a receiver, an agent or an attorney of any person;芒聙聜(repr脙漏sentant personnel)

proxy芒聙聜means a completed and executed form of proxy by means of which a shareholder appoints a proxyholder to attend and act on the shareholder芒聙聶s behalf at a meeting of shareholders;芒聙聜(procuration)
proxyholder芒聙聜means the person appointed by proxy to attend and act on behalf of a shareholder at a meeting of shareholders;芒聙聜(fond脙漏 de pouvoir)
real property芒聙聜includes a leasehold interest in real property;芒聙聜(biens immeubles)

residential property芒聙聜means real property consisting of buildings that are used, or are to be used, to the extent of at least one half of the floor space thereof, as one or more private dwellings;芒聙聜(immeuble r脙漏sidentiel)

securities underwriter芒聙聜means a person who, as principal, agrees to purchase securities with a view to the distribution of the securities or who, as agent for a body corporate or other person, offers for sale or sells securities in connection with a distribution of the securities, and includes a person who participates, directly or indirectly, in a distribution of securities, other than a person whose interest in the distribution of securities is limited to receiving a distributor芒聙聶s or seller芒聙聶s commission payable by a securities underwriter;芒聙聜(souscripteur 脙聽 forfait)

security interest芒聙聜means an interest in or charge on property by way of mortgage, lien, pledge or otherwise taken by a creditor or guarantor to secure the payment or performance of an obligation;芒聙聜(s脙禄ret脙漏)
trade, in respect of securities, means any sale or disposition of securities for valuable consideration;芒聙聜(op脙漏ration)

Definition.Text: fiduciary芒聙聜means any person who is acting in a fiduciary capacity or who has an obligation of honesty and loyalty to another person because they are in a relationship of trust and confidence with that person, and includes a personal representative of a deceased person;芒聙聜(repr脙漏sentant)

proxy芒聙聜means a completed and executed or, in Quebec, signed form of proxy by means of which a shareholder appoints a proxyholder to attend and act on the shareholder芒聙聶s behalf at a meeting of shareholders;芒聙聜(procuration)

residential property芒聙聜means any real property or immovable consisting of buildings of which at least one half of the floor space is used, or is to be used, as one or more private dwellings;芒聙聜(immeuble r脙漏sidentiel)

security interest芒聙聜means an interest or right in or a charge on property, by way of mortgage or hypothec, lien, pledge or otherwise, taken by a creditor or guarantor to secure the payment or performance of an obligation;芒聙聜(s脙禄ret脙漏)

Subparagraph.Text: a company that is a trust company pursuant to subsection 57(2) or a trust company incorporated by or under the laws of a province, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a lawyer or notary acting on behalf of the person referred to in paragraph 96(2)(a), or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of transmission by notarial will in the Province of Quebec, a copy thereof authenticated pursuant to the laws of that Province,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: together with

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an affidavit or declaration of transmission made by the person referred to in paragraph 96(2)(a) that states the particulars of the transmission, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the security certificate that was owned by the deceased holder

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a transfer to the person referred to in paragraph 96(2)(a), with or without the endorsement of that person, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a transfer to any other person, endorsed in accordance with section 114,

ContinuedParagraph.Text: and accompanied by any assurance the company may require under section 130.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 99(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Despite subsection (1), if the laws of the jurisdiction governing the transmission of a security of a deceased holder do not require any of the documents mentioned in paragraphs (1)(a) to (c) in respect of the transmission, a personal representative of the deceased holder is entitled, subject to Part VII and any applicable law relating to the collection of taxes, to become registered as the owner of the security or to designate a person to be registered as the owner, if the personal representative delivers to the company or its transfer agent the following documents:

Notwithstanding subsection (1), if the laws of the jurisdiction governing the transmission of a security of a deceased holder do not require a grant of probate or of letters of administration in respect of the transmission, a personal representative of the deceased holder is entitled, subject to Part VII and any applicable law relating to the collection of taxes, to become registered as the owner or to designate a person to be registered as the owner, if the personal representative delivers to the company or its transfer agent the following documents, namely,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: reasonable proof of the governing laws, of the deceased holder芒聙聶s interest in the security and of the right of the personal representative or the designated person to become the registered shareholder.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 99(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: reasonable proof of the governing laws, of the deceased holder芒聙聶s interest or right in the security and of the right of the personal representative or the designated person to become the registered shareholder.

Section.Text: Subsection 103(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Even against a purchaser for value and without notice of a defect going to the validity of a security, the terms of the security include those stated on the security and those incorporated by reference to another document, instrument or act, statute, rule, regulation or order to the extent that the terms so referred to do not conflict with the stated terms, but the reference is not of itself notice to a purchaser for value of a defect going to the validity of the security, even if the security expressly states that a person accepting it admits the notice.

Even against a purchaser for value and without notice of a defect going to the validity of a security, the terms of the security include those stated on the security and those incorporated therein by reference to another instrument, statute, rule, regulation or order to the extent that the terms so referred to do not conflict with the stated terms, but such a reference is not of itself notice to a purchaser for value of a defect going to the validity of the security, notwithstanding that the security expressly states that a person accepting it admits the notice.

Section.Text: Subsection 107(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A purchaser acquires rights only to the extent of the interest or right purchased.
A purchaser of a limited interest acquires rights only to the extent of the interest purchased.

Section.Text: Subsection 111(5) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A securities broker gives to the broker芒聙聶s customer, to the issuer and to a purchaser, as the case may be, the warranties provided in subsections (1) to (4) and has the rights and privileges of a purchaser under those subsections, and those warranties of and in favour of the broker acting as an agent or mandatary are in addition to warranties given by the broker芒聙聶s customer and warranties given in favour of the broker芒聙聶s customer.

A securities broker gives to the broker芒聙聶s customer, to the issuer and to a purchaser, as the case may be, the warranties provided in subsections (1) to (4) and has the rights and privileges of a purchaser under those subsections, and those warranties of and in favour of the broker acting as an agent are in addition to warranties given by the broker芒聙聶s customer and warranties given in favour of the broker芒聙聶s customer.

Subsection.Text: In this section, section 114, subsections 121(1), 124(4) and 129(1) and section 133, appropriate person means

Paragraph.Label: (e)

Paragraph.Label: (g)

Section.Text: Section 117 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Effect of failure by fiduciary to comply
Section.Text: Failure of a fiduciary to comply with a controlling instrument or act or with the law of the jurisdiction governing the fiduciary relationship, including any law requiring the fiduciary to obtain court approval of a transfer, does not render the fiduciary芒聙聶s endorsement unauthorized for the purposes of this Part.

Failure of a fiduciary to comply with a controlling instrument or with the law of the jurisdiction governing the fiduciary relationship, including any law requiring the fiduciary to obtain court approval of a transfer, does not render the fiduciary芒聙聶s endorsement unauthorized for the purposes of this Part.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 124(4) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

A transfer or pledge of a security shown in the records of a clearing agency or of an interest or right in such a security may, in addition to other methods, be effected by making an appropriate entry in those records if the security is evidenced by
If a security shown in the records of a clearing agency is evidenced by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a security certificate in the custody of the clearing agency or a custodian, or a nominee of either, subject to the instructions of the clearing agency, and is in bearer form or endorsed in blank by an appropriate person or registered in the name of the clearing agency or a custodian, or of a nominee of either, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an uncertificated security registered or recorded in records maintained by or on behalf of the company in the name of the clearing agency or a custodian, or of a nominee of either, subject to the instructions of the clearing agency,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: then, in addition to other methods, a transfer or pledge of the security or any interest therein may be effected by the making of an appropriate entry in the records of the clearing agency.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 124(4) of the English version of the Act after paragraph (b) is repealed.
Subsection 124(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Interests or rights in fungible bulk
Subsection.Text: Under subsections (4) to (10), entries may be in respect of similar securities or interests or rights in similar securities as part of a fungible bulk and may refer merely to a quantity of a particular security without reference to the name of the registered owner, certificate or bond number or any other information and, in appropriate cases, may be on a net basis taking into account other transfers or pledges of the same security.

Under subsections (4) to (10), entries may be in respect of like securities or interests therein as part of a fungible bulk and may refer merely to a quantity of a particular security without reference to the name of the registered owner, certificate or bond number or the like and, in appropriate cases, may be on a net basis taking into account other transfers or pledges of the same security.

A transfer or pledge under subsections (4) to (10) has the effect of a delivery of a security in bearer form or duly endorsed in blank representing the amount of the obligation or the number of shares or rights transferred or pledged.

If a pledge or the creation of a security interest is intended, the making of entries has the effect of a taking of delivery by the pledgee or a secured party and the pledgee or secured party shall be deemed to have taken possession for all purposes.
Subsections 124(6) and (7) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transfert ou gage 脙漏quivaut 脙聽 livraison
Subsection.Text: Le transfert ou le gage pr脙漏vu aux paragraphes (4) 脙聽 (10) 脙漏quivaut 脙聽 la livraison d芒聙聶une valeur mobili脙篓re au porteur ou d脙禄ment endoss脙漏e en blanc et repr脙漏sente soit le montant de l芒聙聶obligation, soit le nombre d芒聙聶actions ou de droits transf脙漏r脙漏s ou mis en gage.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Inscription 脙漏quivaut 脙聽 acceptation de livraison
Subsection.Text: Si le gage ou la cr脙漏ation d芒聙聶une s脙禄ret脙漏 est envisag脙漏, l芒聙聶inscription 脙漏quivaut 脙聽 une acceptation de la livraison par le cr脙漏ancier gagiste ou le cr脙漏ancier garanti et ces derniers sont r脙漏put脙漏s, 脙聽 toutes fins, en avoir pris possession.
Subsection 124(9) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:
Le transfert ou le gage effectu脙漏 en vertu des paragraphes (4) 脙聽 (10) ne constitue pas une inscription de transfert au sens des articles 129 脙聽 136.
A transfer or pledge under subsections (4) to (10) does not constitute a registration of transfer under sections 129 to 136.

Section.Text: Subsection 126(2) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: L芒聙聶acqu脙漏reur peut refuser le transfert ou en demander la r脙漏solution si le c脙漏dant ne se conforme pas, dans un d脙漏lai raisonnable, 脙聽 toute demande faite en vertu du paragraphe (1).
If a transferor fails to comply with a demand under subsection (1) within a reasonable time, the purchaser may reject or rescind the transfer.

Section.Text: Sections 127 and 128 of the Act are replaced by the following:
No seizure of a security or other interest or right evidenced by a security is effective until the person making the seizure obtains possession of the security.

Section.MarginalNote: No liability if good faith disposition
Section.Text: An agent or mandatary, or a bailee or depositary, who in good faith, including observance of reasonable commercial standards if they are in the business of buying, selling or otherwise dealing with securities of a company, has received securities and sold, pledged or delivered them according to the instructions of the principal or mandator, or the bailor or depositor, is not liable for conversion or for participation in breach of fiduciary duty even though the principal or mandator, or the bailor or depositor has no right to dispose of the securities.
No seizure of a security or other interest evidenced thereby is effective until the person making the seizure obtains possession of the security.

An agent or bailee who in good faith, including observance of reasonable commercial standards if the agent or bailee is in the business of buying, selling or otherwise dealing with securities of a company, has received securities and sold, pledged or delivered them according to the instructions of the agent芒聙聶s or bailee芒聙聶s principal is not liable for conversion or for participation in breach of fiduciary duty even though the principal has no right to dispose of the securities.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 130(1)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)

Subsection.Text: An issuer may discharge a duty of inquiry by any reasonable means, including notifying an adverse claimant by registered mail sent to the address provided by the adverse claimant or, if no such address has been provided, to the adverse claimant芒聙聶s residence or regular place of business, that a security has been presented for registration of transfer by a named person and that the transfer will be registered unless, within thirty days after the date of mailing of the notice, either

Section.Text: The portion of section 137 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
An authenticating trustee, registrar, transfer agent or other agent or mandatary of an issuer has, in respect of the issue, registration of transfer and cancellation of a security of the issuer,

Subsection 146(6) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: La soci脙漏t脙漏 ou la personne agissant en son nom n芒聙聶engage pas sa responsabilit脙漏 en diffusant une proposition ou une d脙漏claration conform脙漏ment aux paragraphes (2) et (3).

Subsections 160.02(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

A shareholder who is entitled to vote at a meeting of shareholders may, by executing or, in Quebec, signing a form of proxy, appoint a proxyholder or one or more alternate proxyholders, who are not required to be shareholders, to attend and act at the meeting in the manner and to the extent authorized by the proxy and with the authority conferred by the proxy.
A form of proxy is to be executed or, in Quebec, signed by a shareholder or by a shareholder芒聙聶s attorney authorized in writing to do so.

A shareholder who is entitled to vote at a meeting of shareholders may, by executing a form of proxy, appoint a proxyholder or one or more alternate proxyholders, who are not required to be shareholders, to attend and act at the meeting in the manner and to the extent authorized by the proxy and with the authority conferred by the proxy.

A form of proxy must indicate, in bold-face type, that the shareholder by whom or on whose behalf it is executed may appoint a proxyholder, other than a person designated in the form of proxy, to attend and act on the shareholder芒聙聶s behalf at a meeting to which the proxy relates, and must contain instructions as to the manner in which the shareholder may do so.

Section.Text: Section 160.03 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

The directors may specify, in a notice calling a meeting of shareholders or a continuation of a meeting of shareholders after an adjournment, a time before which proxies to be used at the meeting or the continued meeting must be deposited with the company or its transfer agent. The time specified must not be more than 48 hours, excluding Saturdays and holidays, before the meeting or the continued meeting.

The directors may specify, in a notice calling a meeting of shareholders or a continuation of a meeting of shareholders after an adjournment, a time before which executed forms of proxy to be used at the meeting or the continued meeting must be deposited with the company or its transfer agent. The time specified must not be more than forty-eight hours, excluding Saturdays and holidays, before the meeting or the continued meeting.
Subsection 160.06(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A person who solicits a proxy and is appointed proxyholder must attend in person or cause an alternate proxyholder to attend every meeting in respect of which the proxy is valid, and the proxyholder or alternate proxyholder must comply with the directions of the shareholder who appointed them.

A person who solicits a proxy and is appointed proxyholder shall attend in person or cause an alternate proxyholder to attend every meeting in respect of which the proxy is valid, and the proxyholder or alternate proxyholder shall comply with the directions of the shareholder who executed the form of proxy.

Section.Text: Subsection 160.07(4) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

No company or person acting on its behalf incurs any liability by reason only of circulating a director芒聙聶s statement in compliance with subsection (1).

Section.Text: Subsection 203(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If authorized to do so by a special resolution, the directors of a company that is a trust company pursuant to subsection 57(2) may delegate, with or without the power of sub-delegation, to the chief executive officer of the company, the exercise of all or any of the powers or authorities of the company, whether discretionary or otherwise, arising out of any will, trust, deed, contract or other instrument or act creating a trust.

Where authorized to do so by a special resolution, the directors of a company that is a trust company pursuant to subsection 57(2) may delegate, with or without the power of sub-delegation, to the chief executive officer of the company, the exercise of all or any of the powers or authorities of the company, whether discretionary or otherwise, arising out of any will, trust, deed, contract or other instrument creating a trust.

Section.Text: Subsection 215(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: When a director of a company pays a debt referred to in subsection (1) that is proven in liquidation and dissolution or winding-up proceedings, the director is entitled to any preference that the employee would have been entitled to and, if a judgment has been obtained, the director is

Section.Text: Section 227 of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Les modifications de l芒聙聶acte constitutif ou des r脙篓glements administratifs ne portent pas atteinte aux causes d芒聙聶actions ou aux r脙漏clamations d脙漏j脙聽 n脙漏es pouvant engager la soci脙漏t脙漏, ses administrateurs ou ses dirigeants, ni aux possibilit脙漏s de poursuite pour infraction, ni aux proc脙漏dures civiles, p脙漏nales ou administratives auxquelles ils sont parties.

No amendment to the incorporating instrument or by-laws of a company affects an existing cause of action or claim or liability to prosecution in favour of or against the company or its directors or officers, or any civil, criminal or administrative action or proceeding to which the company or any of its directors or officers are a party.
Subsection 234.1(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: If a company or any director, any officer, any employee or any agent or mandatary of a company is contravening or has failed to comply with any term or condition made in respect of the issuance of letters patent of amalgamation, the Minister may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order directing the company or the director, the officer, the employee or the agent or mandatary to comply with the term or condition, cease the contravention or do any thing that is required to be done, and on the application the court may so order and make any other order it thinks fit.

If a company or any director, officer, employee or agent of a company is contravening or has failed to comply with any term or condition made in respect of the issuance of letters patent of amalgamation, the Minister may, in addition to any other action that may be taken under this Act, apply to a court for an order directing the company or the director, officer, employee or agent to comply with the term or condition, cease the contravention or do any thing that is required to be done, and on the application the court may so order and make any other order it thinks fit.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 245(5) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person requiring a company to supply a basic list of shareholders may, if the person states in the accompanying affidavit that supplemental lists are required, request the company or its agent or mandatary, on payment of a reasonable fee, to provide supplemental lists of shareholders setting out any changes from the basic list in the names and addresses of the shareholders and the number of shares owned by each shareholder for each business day following the date to which the basic list is made up.

A person requiring a company to supply a basic list of shareholders may, if the person states in the accompanying affidavit that supplemental lists are required, request the company or its agent, on payment of a reasonable fee, to provide supplemental lists of shareholders setting out any changes from the basic list in the names and addresses of the shareholders and the number of shares owned by each shareholder for each business day following the date to which the basic list is made up.
The portion of subsection 245(6) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: When supplemental lists to be furnished
Subsection.Text: A company or its agent or mandatary must provide a supplemental list of shareholders required under subsection (5)
A company or its agent shall provide a supplemental list of shareholders required under subsection (5)

Section.Text: The portion of section 249 of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A company and its agents or mandataries must take reasonable precautions to
A company and its agents shall take reasonable precautions to

Subsection.Text: Subsection 253(6) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person who wishes to examine the central securities register, take extracts from it or have copies of it made may, on payment of a reasonable fee, if they state in the accompanying affidavit that supplementary information is required, request the company or its agent or mandatary to provide supplementary information setting out any changes made to the register.

A person who wishes to examine a central securities register, take extracts from it or have copies of it made may on payment of a reasonable fee, if they state in the accompanying affidavit that supplementary information is required, request the company or its agent to provide supplementary information setting out any changes made to the register.
The portion of subsection 253(7) of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: When supplementary information to be provided
Subsection.Text: A company or its agent or mandatary must provide the supplementary information within
A company or its agent shall provide the supplementary information within

Section.Text: Section 255 of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
A company may appoint an agent or mandatary to maintain its central securities register and each of its branch securities registers.
A company may appoint an agent to maintain its central securities register and each of its branch securities registers.
The portion of section 259 of the English version of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
A company, its agent or mandatary or a trustee as defined in section 299 is not required to produce
A company, its agent or a trustee within the meaning of section 299 is not required to produce
Subsection 261(2) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A document executed or, in Quebec, signed on behalf of a company is not invalid merely because a corporate seal is not affixed to it.
A document executed on behalf of a company is not invalid merely because a corporate seal is not affixed to it.

Section.Text: The definitions option d芒聙聶achat and option de vente in subsection 270(1) of the French version of the Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: option d芒聙聶achat芒聙聜Option n脙漏gociable par livraison qui permet d芒聙聶exiger que soit livr脙漏 un nombre pr脙漏cis d芒聙聶actions 脙聽 un prix et dans un d脙漏lai d脙漏termin脙漏s. Est exclu de la pr脙漏sente d脙漏finition l芒聙聶option ou le droit d芒聙聶acqu脙漏rir des actions de la personne morale qui l芒聙聶accorde.芒聙聜(call)
option de vente芒聙聜Option n脙漏gociable par livraison qui permet de livrer un nombre pr脙漏cis d芒聙聶actions 脙聽 un prix et dans un d脙漏lai d脙漏termin脙漏s.芒聙聜(put)

call芒聙聜means an option, transferable by delivery, to demand delivery of a specified number or amount of shares at a fixed price within a specified time but does not include an option or right to acquire shares of the body corporate that granted the option or right to acquire; 芒聙聜(option d芒聙聶achat)
put芒聙聜means an option, transferable by delivery, to deliver a specified number or amount of shares at a fixed price within a specified time; 芒聙聜(option de vente)

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a trust or an estate or succession in which the person has a substantial beneficial interest or in respect of which they serve as a trustee, an executor or administrator of the estate or a liquidator of the succession or in a similar capacity;

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: tous les actionnaires opposants dont la soci脙漏t脙漏 n芒聙聶a pas achet脙漏 les actions sont joints comme parties 脙聽 l芒聙聶instance et sont li脙漏s par la d脙漏cision du tribunal;

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all dissenting shareholders whose shares have not been purchased by the company are to be joined as parties and are bound by the decision of the court;

Sur demande pr脙漏sent脙漏e en vertu des paragraphes (14) ou (15), le tribunal peut d脙漏cider qu芒聙聶il existe d芒聙聶autres actionnaires opposants 脙聽 joindre comme parties 脙聽 l芒聙聶instance et doit alors fixer la juste valeur des actions en question.

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a trust or an estate or succession in which the offeror has a substantial beneficial interest or in respect of which they serve as a trustee, an executor or administrator of the estate or a liquidator of the succession or in a similar capacity,

Definition.Text: offeror芒聙聜means a person, other than an agent or mandatary, who makes a take-over bid, and includes two or more persons who, directly or indirectly,
offeror芒聙聜means a person, other than an agent, who makes a take-over bid, and includes two or more persons who, directly or indirectly,

Section.Text: Paragraph 295(a) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
Paragraph.Text: tous les pollicit脙漏s opposants qui ont choisi d芒聙聶exiger le paiement de la juste valeur de leurs actions en conformit脙漏 avec l芒聙聶alin脙漏a 291b) et dont les actions n芒聙聶ont pas 脙漏t脙漏 acquises par le pollicitant sont joints comme parties 脙聽 l芒聙聶instance et sont li脙漏s par la d脙漏cision du tribunal;

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all dissenting offerees who have made elections to demand payment under paragraph 291(b) and whose shares have not been acquired by the offeror shall be joined as parties and are bound by the decision of the court; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 296(1) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Avant de fixer la juste valeur des actions de tous les pollicit脙漏s opposants, le tribunal peut, sur demande pr脙漏sent脙漏e conform脙漏ment aux paragraphes 294(1) ou (2), d脙漏cider s芒聙聶il existe d芒聙聶autres pollicit脙漏s opposants 脙聽 joindre comme parties 脙聽 l芒聙聶instance.

On an application to a court under subsection 294(1) or (2), the court may determine whether any other person is a dissenting offeree who should be joined as a party, and the court shall then fix a fair value for the shares of all dissenting offerees.
Paragraph 296(4)(b) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: b)
Paragraph.Text: faire d脙漏tenir en fiducie ou en fid脙漏icommis le montant en num脙漏raire ou toute autre contrepartie par une personne autre que la soci脙漏t脙漏 pollicit脙漏e;

Subsection.Text: In connection with proceedings under subsection 294(1) or (2), a court may make any order it thinks fit and, without limiting the generality of the foregoing, may

Paragraph.Text: 脗聽

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: order that the money or other consideration is to be held in trust by a person other than the offeree company;

Section.Text: The definition trust indenture in section 299 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: trust indenture芒聙聜means any deed, indenture or other instrument or act, including any supplement or amendment made to it by a company, under which the company issues subordinated indebtedness and in which a person is appointed as trustee for the holders of that subordinated indebtedness.芒聙聜(acte de fiducie)

trust indenture芒聙聜means any deed, indenture or other instrument, including any supplement or amendment thereto, made by a company under which the company issues subordinated indebtedness and in which a person is appointed as trustee for the holders of the subordinated indebtedness issued thereunder.芒聙聜(acte de fiducie)

Section.Text: Subparagraph 320(2)(b)(iii) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: has been a liquidator, trustee in bankruptcy, receiver, receiver and manager or sequestrator of any affiliate of the company within the two years immediately preceding the person芒聙聶s proposed appointment as auditor of the company, other than an affiliate that is a subsidiary of the company acquired under section 457 or through a realization of security under section 458.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1),

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person is deemed not to be independent of a company if that person, a business partner of that person or a firm of accountants of which that person is a member

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: has been a liquidator, trustee in bankruptcy, receiver or receiver and manager of any affiliate of the company within the two years immediately preceding the person芒聙聶s proposed appointment as auditor of the company, other than an affiliate that is a subsidiary of the company acquired pursuant to section 457 or through a realization of security pursuant to section 458.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 329(1) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:
On the request of the auditor of a company, the present or former directors, officers, employees or agents or mandataries of the company must, to the extent that they are reasonably able to do so,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: permit access to any records, assets and security held by the company or any entity in which the company has a substantial investment that are, in the auditor芒聙聶s opinion, necessary to enable the auditor to perform their duties; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide any information and explanations that are, in the auditor芒聙聶s opinion, necessary to enable the auditor to perform their duties.

Subsection.Text: On the request of the auditor of a company, the present or former directors, officers, employees or agents of the company shall, to the extent that such persons are reasonably able to do so,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: permit access to such records, assets and security held by the company or any entity in which the company has a substantial investment, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: provide such information and explanations

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: as are, in the opinion of the auditor, necessary to enable the auditor to perform the duties of auditor of the company.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 329(2)(a) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: obtain from the present or former directors, officers, employees and agents or mandataries of any entity in which the company has a substantial investment the information and explanations that those persons are reasonably able to provide and that are, in the auditor芒聙聶s opinion, necessary to enable the auditor to perform their duties; and

Subsection.Text: On the request of the auditor of a company, the directors of the company shall, to the extent that they are reasonably able to do so,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: obtain from the present or former directors, officers, employees and agents of any entity in which the company has a substantial investment the information and explanations that such persons are reasonably able to provide and that are, in the opinion of the auditor, necessary to enable the auditor to perform the duties of auditor of the company; and

Section.Text: Subsection 341(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: An application made or an action brought or intervened in under subsection 339(1) or section 343 must not be stayed, discontinued, settled or dismissed for want of prosecution or, in Quebec, failure to act within a time limit set under the applicable rules of civil procedure, without the approval of the court given on any terms that the court thinks fit and, if the court determines that the interests or rights of any complainant might be substantially affected by any stay, discontinuance, settlement or dismissal, the court may order any party to the application or action to give notice to the complainant.

An application made or an action brought or intervened in under subsection 339(1) or section 343 shall not be stayed, discontinued, settled or dismissed for want of prosecution without the approval of the court given on such terms as the court thinks fit and, if the court determines that the interests of any complainant might be substantially affected by any stay, discontinuance, settlement or dismissal, the court may order any party to the application or action to give notice to the complainant.

Section.Text: Paragraph 350(4)(d) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: d)
Paragraph.Text: si elle est une soci脙漏t脙漏 de fiducie au sens du paragraphe 57(2), faire tous les arrangements n脙漏cessaires pour transf脙漏rer 脙聽 une autre soci脙漏t脙漏 de fiducie au sens de ce paragraphe tous les fonds ou autres 脙漏l脙漏ments d芒聙聶actif qu芒聙聶elle d脙漏tient en fiducie ou en fid脙漏icommis, 脙聽 l芒聙聶exception des 脙漏l脙漏ments d芒聙聶actif d脙漏tenus 脙聽 l芒聙聶脙漏gard des fonds en fiducie garantie;

Subsection.Text: Where the Minister has approved an application made pursuant to section 349 with respect to a company, the company shall

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: where the company is a trust company pursuant to subsection 57(2), make such arrangements as are necessary to transfer to another company that is a trust company pursuant to subsection 57(2) money or other assets held in trust by the company, other than assets held in respect of guaranteed trust money; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of section 354 of the English version of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: If the court is satisfied that the company is able to pay or adequately provide for the discharge of all its obligations and, in the case of a company that is a trust company pursuant to subsection 57(2), make satisfactory arrangements for the protection of persons in relation to whom it is acting in a fiduciary capacity, the court may make any order it thinks fit in connection with the company芒聙聶s liquidation and dissolution including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an order to liquidate;

Section.Text: In connection with the liquidation and dissolution of a company, the court may, where it is satisfied

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that the company is able to pay or adequately provide for the discharge of all its obligations, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a company that is a trust company pursuant to subsection 57(2), that the company is able to make satisfactory arrangements for the protection of persons in relation to whom the company is acting in a fiduciary capacity,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: make any order it thinks fit including, without limiting the generality of the foregoing,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an order to liquidate,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: an order appointing a liquidator, with or without security, fixing a liquidator芒聙聶s remuneration and replacing a liquidator,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an order appointing inspectors or referees, specifying their powers, fixing their remuneration and replacing inspectors or referees,

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: an order determining the notice to be given to any interested person, or dispensing with notice to any person,

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: an order determining the validity of any claims made against the company,

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: an order, at any stage of the proceedings, restraining the directors and officers of the company from

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: exercising any of their powers, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: collecting or receiving any debt or other property of the company, and from paying out or transferring any property of the company, except as permitted by the court,

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: an order determining and enforcing the duty or liability of any present or former director, officer or shareholder

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to the company, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: for an obligation of the company,

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: an order approving the payment, satisfaction or compromise of claims against the company and the retention of assets for that purpose, and determining the adequacy of provisions for the payment, discharge or transfer of any trust obligation or other obligation of the company, whether liquidated, unliquidated, future or contingent,

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: with the concurrence of the Superintendent, an order providing for the disposal or destruction of the documents, records or registers of the company,

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: on the application of a creditor, an inspector or the liquidator, an order giving directions on any matter arising in the liquidation,

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: after notice has been given to all interested parties, an order relieving the liquidator from any omission or default on such terms as the court thinks fit and confirming any act of the liquidator,

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: subject to sections 361 to 363, an order approving any proposed, interim or final distribution to shareholders, if any, or incorporators, in money or in property,

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: an order disposing of any property belonging to creditors, shareholders and incorporators who cannot be found,

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: on the application of any director, officer, shareholder, incorporator, creditor or the liquidator,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an order staying the liquidation proceedings on such terms and conditions as the court thinks fit,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an order continuing or discontinuing the liquidation proceedings, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an order to the liquidator to restore to the company all of its remaining property, and

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: after the liquidator has rendered the liquidator芒聙聶s final account to the court, an order directing the company to apply to the Minister for letters patent dissolving the company.

Section.Text: Subsection 362(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A liquidator must give notice of their intention to make an application under subsection (1) to the Superintendent, to each inspector appointed under section 354, to each shareholder of the company or, if there are no shareholders, to each incorporator and to any person who provided a security or a fidelity bond or fidelity insurance for the liquidation.

A liquidator shall give notice of the liquidator芒聙聶s intention to make an application under subsection (1) to the Superintendent, to each inspector appointed under section 354, to each shareholder of the company or, if there are no shareholders, to each incorporator and to any person who provided a security or fidelity bond for the liquidation.

Section.Text: Paragraph 368(4)(a) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: a)
